Field Mapping between Entities in Dynamics 365 CE

Tamilarasu Arunachalam
January 10, 2024 • 5 min read

    You know what!. We can map fields with the related entity without using any client-side script. It can be done by the Out-of-Box feature, which is Entity Field Mapping. But that entities must be related with 1:N relationship. But there are some limitations such as we can map the fields that must be of same type and format. For example, We can map a field from account entity with the contact entity.<p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">    As this kind of field mappings works on the scenarios where we create the child records from parent record. In case of creating a child record independently by the main form or any other way, the entity field mapping doesn’t work. I have created the entity field mapping by following the below steps.</p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><ul><li>Create a new custom field in Account and Contact entity. And I have created a text field and named it Test Mapping Field.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><ul><li>As the account has 1:N relationship with contact. We don’t need to do anything with the relationship here. If you are using a custom entity, you need to create a 1:N relationship between them.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><ul><li>Switch to the classic mode, Navigate to Account Entity 🡲 Click on the 1:N relationship(contact_customer_accounts)</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><ul><li>After opening the relationship, click on the mapping on the left side pane.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><ul><li>In the Mapping page, Create a new mapping by clicking on the new button</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><ul><li>A pop-up for creating a field mapping opens up, and select fields to map. I will map my custom field from account to contact.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><ul><li>Navigate to the form editor for Accounts Entity 🡲 Edit the Main form 🡲 Add the field to the form, then save and publish.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><p style="text-align: justify;"></p><p></p><p></p><ul><li>Navigate to the form editor for Contacts Entity 🡲 Edit the Quick Create form 🡲 Add the field to the form, then save and publish.</li></ul><p></p><p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: center;"></p><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: left;"><p></p><p></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Add any value to the custom field which we added in accounts form. Then save the record.</li></ul><p></p><p></p></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: left;"><p></p><p></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Create a new Contact from the contact subgrid form Account main form.</li></ul><p></p><p></p></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both;"></div></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: left;"><p></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>You will find the value which we gave in the Account Main form will come over to the Contact create form.</li></ul><p></p></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><p style="text-align: left;">By this way, we can achieve the mapping of fields with the related entity using Out-of-the-Box feature (without coding). You can refer the Microsoft Documentation on Entity field mapping here</p></div><div>
</div><div><div style="text-align: center;">Have a good day!</div></div>
